reasoning: As of early November 2025 (the knowledge cutoff), the price of gold is hovering around $4,000 per ounce, having recently hit a record of $4,250 in mid-October. The market resolves YES if the COMEX Gold Continuous Contract closes at $3,200 or greater on the final trading day of 2025 (less than two months away). For gold to fall below $3,200 by year-end, it would require a precipitous 20% crash in just 50 days. Given that most bearish estimates for the rest of 2025 do not see gold dropping below $3,700, and standard option pricing models (Black-Scholes) place the probability of a 20% short-term drop at comfortably less than 2-3%, the asset is overwhelmingly likely to finish the year above $3,200. I cap the assessment at 0.95 according to the maximum confidence constraint.
